desc: First stop. Munich showed us people come with empty bags and leave with two full ones. MTC Halle went off.
Three days at Kühlhaus. Best DJs, 90s fits for €10, energy that doesn't need explaining.
Under the railway arches at Oberhafen. Biggest vintage haul of the tour so far.
Westergas — Europe's most beautiful industrial park. Live techno, vintage market, specialty coffee. The premium drop everyone's talking about.
The coolest city in the Netherlands. HAKA Gebouw is a concrete monster with brutal acoustics. Amsterdam's B-side — and the better one.
Cologne's underground goes its own way. Halle Tor 2: industrial, honest, no pretense. Summer starts here.
Paris already has a scene. We bring vintage that doesn't cost a kidney, and artists you'll know before everyone else.
France's food capital has a clubbing scene the rest of Europe sleeps on. We're about to fix that.
Madrid in August — right when nobody expects us. The Iberian premium drop. Best vintage market of the season.
Post-Sónar, pre-autumn. Barcelona at its most relaxed and coolest. Palo Alto is vintage by nature.
LX Factory — the converted plant at the heart of Lisbon's cultural scene. Premium drop southernmost of the season.
Porto keeps it real in everything. Freixo on the river with the AW drop just landed.
Scandinavia, finally. Copenhagen is the global capital of sustainable design — our kind of city. Refshaleøen, the island that changed the game.
The other side of the Øresund bridge. Malmö has Sweden's most honest scene — no Stockholm prices, double the energy.
We showed up. Eriksberg in autumn with the best Swedish DJs and the most necessary secondhand coat drop of the tour.
Second visit. Amsterdam on Halloween — costumes are vintage by definition. NDSM Wharf on the tour for the first time.
Vienna is more underground than it looks. Marx Halle: 100 years of history. Premium drop at the heart of Central Europe.
MeetFactory — Cerný's converted factory. Prague has the most underrated art scene in Europe. Long overdue.
Poland waited long enough. Fabryka Norblin is the most impressive rehabilitated industrial complex on the continent.
Year-end closer. Kraftwerk Berlin for the last premium drop of 2026. The year ends where techno hits hardest in the world.
Milan Fashion Week has competition. We bring vintage at €10 when they charge €500 to sit front row.
OGR — Officine Grandi Riparazioni — one of Italy's best art and music spaces. Nobody talks about it. We do.
Switzerland, expensive in everything except us. Halle 622 with the dopest winter collection of the tour.
Belgium is the birthplace of European techno and it knows it. Hangar Brussels with a lineup that respects history.
Third visit — there's a reason. Paradiso, the temple. Spring drop just landed.
Frankfurt isn't just finance. Brotfabrik has been the alternative cultural heart of the city for 30 years.
One year after the first event. Back where it all started. Alte Kongresshalle — the most beautiful space we've ever been in.
Madrid twice in the same season — because they asked. New spring drop, most interesting DJs in the Spanish scene right now.
The south joins the tour. Sevilla has an energy that doesn't exist anywhere else.
Back to Lyon with La Sucrière at Quai Rambaud. Spring on the Rhône, outdoor summer market.
Second visit to Copenhagen. Vega — Scandinavia's most iconic concert hall — for the Nordic season close.
Berlin three times — it's a serious relationship now. Arena Berlin on the Spree for summer.
Fàbrica Moritz in Eixample — a converted brewery turned cultural space. Barcelona summer always wants more.
Season finale. Madrid for the last premium drop — the biggest of the year. Everything we've learned in 34 stops. See you there.
